Cookie Policy

Last Updated: March 24, 2026

Consistent with our vision focused on high-end technology and strict architectural principles, the Harezm corporate website rejects the philosophy of aggressive targeted marketing codes and adheres strictly to the "Minimal and Analytical Data" principle.

This policy explains the technical reasons and limitations for the utilization of small data files (cookies) that may be sent to your browser when you examine our website.

1. What is a Cookie?

Cookies are minute memory fragments stored by your internet browser (Google Chrome, Safari, Edge, etc.) on your computer, tablet, or mobile device during your connection. Their sole objective is to allow complex websites to function efficiently and to locally memorize user-specific interface settings (for example: Dark/Light theme mode persistence).

2. Categories of Cookies Used by Us

Our infrastructure deploys only the essentially required files classified into two specific categories:

shield Strictly Necessary (System) Cookies

These are critical technical elements needed for the reliable performance of the website's modern framework. They are mainly responsible for fetching out your preferred operating system mode (Dark/Light mode detection keys), preserving translation states, and handling secure network routing tasks. They cannot be toggled off and they don't scrape your personal identity.

analytics Analytic Performance Cookies

Used internally to generate generic traffic flow analysis—to identify whether clients prefer exploring the SAP TRM documentation over S/4HANA solutions—and to accordingly refine server efficiency. All metric records gathered translate strictly into anonymized analytical datasets.

3. Which Cookies DO WE NOT Use?

Contrary to the broader corporate sector, it lies at the core of our technical policy that third-party profiling, advertising, and invasive re-targeting scripts (e.g. Google Ads/Analytics trackers, Facebook Pixels) are strictly non-existent on our platform. We don't trace individuals to enforce display advertisements elsewhere relying on past digital footprints.

4. Managing Your Cookie Settings

If you firmly wish to prevent any local storage interaction, you hold the definitive control to wipe existing cookies and block future acceptances via the privacy configurations embedded in your web browser:

  • Google Chrome: Hardware Options > Privacy and Security > Cookies tab.
  • Safari: Preferences > Privacy > Block all cookies switch.
  • Firefox: Options > Privacy > Cookies menu.

Note: Blocking necessary system cookies entirely may trigger slight interface disruptions on our site; the color theme synchronizations or language logic may reset across manual page transitions.
